active_conjecture.summary: The active conjecture is no longer about the fixed stable family. It asks whether bounded-support whether a third bounded-support observable that repairs the certified n=8 repeated-root collisions can extend uniformly across sampled partition labels with inverse-polynomial gaps, and feed a branch-weighted frame whose outcomes decode the hidden involution. Finite variance and covariance rank are only filters.

execution_model: Research generation and experiments run interactively during active Codex sessions. A GitHub workflow validates pushed snapshots, and a scheduled backup can commit only validation-passing changes; neither mechanism performs autonomous quantum-algorithm research between sessions.

milestones.detail: Shape, first-stage, and second-stage observables give all 25 encoded labels on both trees and a polynomial left/right relabelling isometry.
milestones.title: The stable coupling-tree interface is complete

milestones.detail: Fifty-four exact character-ratio inequalities prove lambda_min(F)>=(71/825)n^-5 and polynomial inverse-square-root filters for two involution families.
milestones.title: The stable three-copy frame is filterable

milestones.detail: Its exact probability is d_W^3 d_xi Tr(F)/(n!)^3, at most (25/3)n^9/(n!)^3; postselection and generic amplification are superpolynomial.
milestones.title: Natural access kills the fixed stable branch

milestones.detail: For fixed K, total weak-Fourier mass is at most 2 P_K n^(2K)/n!. Uniform adaptation to typical high-dimensional partitions is now mandatory.
milestones.title: Every fixed bounded-tail route is cut

milestones.detail: TC2/TT1 and its disjoint extension are exactly degenerate at n=8. Exact parameterized fourth moments instead prove TT1+c*TC1 has simple spectrum for every c!=0 on 6 n=8 targets of multiplicity at most four, including both known collisions. Exact quotient transfer at c=1 extends this to 20/20 targets through multiplicity 17. Degree-28 transfer and class-Fourier contraction extend fixed-c1 separation to 27/27 n=9 targets. All-n, gap, transform, and decoder theorems remain open.
milestones.title: An independent third generator repairs the known finite collisions

next_actions.detail: The fixed separator survives all 27 n=9 targets and the first 2 n=10 targets, but explicit n=10 contraction reaches 91.7 GB by degree five. Derive a recurrence before extending the ladder.
next_actions.title: Attack the all-n separator conjecture

next_actions.detail: Aggregate over naturally sampled labels instead of postselecting sectors, and prove the retained mass remains inverse polynomial.
next_actions.title: Keep natural branch mass in every theorem

next_actions.detail: Specify the hidden-involution-dependent measurement distribution, construct a decoder, and try to reproduce it with classical character and tensor methods.
next_actions.title: Derive and attack an outcome law

overview: The project is a proof and falsification engine, not an algorithm demo. Its strongest constructive result is a polynomially filterable three-copy stable frame. Its strongest negative result is more important: every predetermined bounded-tail Fourier family has factorially small natural mass. The nonabelian route must now turn a finite generator portfolio into an all-n, jointly gapped, decodable transform; the single-generator and first two-generator spans have both been exactly falsified, while fixed-coefficient TC1 now has an exact simple-spectrum certificate on all 20 n=8 targets.

tracks.evidence: The all-target census evaluated millions of legal targets; the remaining question is a source-conditioned reduced-basis theorem, not another target sweep.
tracks.next: Prove a random-label LLL geometry law or abandon nearest-plane branching.
tracks.summary: Exact source models, sieve accounting, lattice attacks, and marker-aware decoders were implemented. Fixed-depth nearest-plane lists eventually collapse, so small-n decoder success is not evidence of a polynomial algorithm.
tracks.title: Hidden shift / DHSP

tracks.evidence: Current triage retains 0 viable code rows; the last Goppa syzygy collision was resolved by the hull-projector route.
tracks.next: Generate a new natural family that survives the complete classical triage gate.
tracks.summary: Goppa, quasi-cyclic, finite-geometry, rank-metric, and CFI-derived rows were attacked with support, hull, Schur, syzygy, projector, recovery, and graph-side invariants.
tracks.title: Code equivalence

tracks.evidence: Encoded labels: 25/25. All-n conditioning families: 2. Naturally accessible fixed branches: 0. At n=20 the typical-source audit reaches multiplicity 6,408,361. Finite typical non-scalar coverage: 32/32 blocks; covariance-rank>=2: 29; dense n=7 simple tableau blocks: 224; common finite support-three coefficient rules: 4; class-compressed scalar blocks through n=10: 7; two-generator finite coverage: 108/108; repeated-root collision targets: 2; disjoint-extension repeated-root targets: 2; TC1-repaired collision targets: 2; exact n=8 low-multiplicity coverage: 6/20; fixed-c1 full n=8 coverage: 20/20; certified n=8 normalized minimum gap: 0.00125042; fixed-c1 full n=9 coverage: 27/27; certified n=9 normalized minimum gap: 0.000214586; n=10 first-target coverage: 2/40; uniform gap theorems: 0.
tracks.next: Derive an all-n class-algebra recurrence and normalized gap bound, while probing n=10 for the first collision or severe gap collapse.
tracks.status: Finite n=8 and n=9 separators proved; algorithm blocked
tracks.summary: One fixed stable family now has complete encoded coupling-tree labels, a direct three-copy frame block encoding, an all-n eigenvalue lower bound, and polynomial inverse filters. Exact access accounting cuts every fixed bounded-tail extension. Exact character moments now show non-scalar bounded-support action on typical blocks, but a fourth-moment certificate kills the first two-generator resolver.
tracks.title: Nonabelian coset states

verdict.detail: Every speedup claim remains blocked. A complete 25-label stable coupling-tree interface, direct frame block encoding, all-n conditioning bound, and polynomial inverse filter are proved. The same audit then cuts this branch as an algorithmic route: its natural-input probability is factorially small.
verdict.title: No breakthrough yet
